message: Node started: Let S be the set of all positive integers n. Let f(n) be the Hamming weight of the product 2023 * n, defined as the number of set bits in the binary representation of 2023 * n. Let C = 2023. Given the prime factorization of C, determine the existence of a minimum value m such that m = min {f(n) | n is in S}. Specifically, evaluate if there exists an n in S such that f(n) = 1, and if not, determine the smallest integer m > 1 for which the set {n in S | f(n) = m} is non-empty, subject to the constraint that 2023 * n must be representable in binary.
